Title: Deaf Mute Girl Reciting "Star Spangled Banner"
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *As implied by the title, this is simply a performance of the national anthem. The woman who signs the national anthem uses American Sign Language (ASL).

Title: Martyrs of the Alamo (aka Birth of Texas)
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Based on the real Deaf Smith... the character in this film feigns deafness... Smith makes the sign for deaf... fires a rifle behind Smith, to which he fails to respond.

Title: The Silent Voice
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Bushman plays musician Franklyn Starr, who loses his hearing... becomes a misanthrope... He eventually recovers his hearing.

Title: Menace of the Mute
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): From the Ashton Kirk, Investigator, series. The murderer is mute. Kirk finds one of the notes the mute has written...

Title: The Dumb Bandit
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Ford is a sheepherder who dons a mask and reappears as a 'dumb' bandit to test the loyalty of his wife.

Title: The Dummy
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): A young man (Pickford) to masquerade as a 'wealthy deaf and dumb boy' as bait for kidnappers. Pickford eventually rescues the girl.

Title: Deliverance
Deaf Character(s) Played By: Etna Ross; Ann Mason; Helen Keller
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Subtitled 'The Most Wonderful Woman in the World, Helen Keller'... The film contrasts the lives of Keller and her girlhood friend, Nadja.

Title: The Miracle Man
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): A gang of fakes uses a deaf-blind man to 'heal' them... Lon Chaney plays a fake cripple, Frog... A young boy is actually cured.

Title: Bits of Life
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): One episode, 'The Man Who Heard Everything,' depicts the life of a deaf barber, who receives a hearing-aid device... discovers that his friends are corrupt and his wife is unfaithful.

Title: A Voice in the Dark
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *A deaf witness to a murder accuses a young woman... a blind witness correctly identifies the guilty party... Norman's screen credit reads: 'Deaf, and with an irritable disposition'.

Title: The Man Who Played God
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): This film appeared in four versions... a concert pianist who loses his hearing... learns to lip-read... In the end he falls and regains his hearing.

Title: The Hunchback of Notre Dame
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This is the classic story of Quasimodo (Chaney)... the hunchback Quasimodo is deafened because of his job as bellringer... cannot understand the questions of an equally deafened judge (Cossar).

Title: The Silent Stranger
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): A secret service agent named Jack Taylor, disguised as a deaf-mute, investigates a series of thefts of the U.S. Mail.

Title: Siege
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): The 'pathetic mute' nephew likes the daughter-in-law and sends her flowers, but he commits suicide after his family finds out and ridicules him.

Title: His Busy Hour
Studio/Producer: Independently produced by James Spearing and Bertha Lincoln Heustis
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): There is no evidence that this film was ever distributed commercially.

Title: You'd Be Surprised
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Raymond Griffith stars as the coroner-sleuth... a deaf valet, who witnessed the murder; in a surprise ending, the deaf valet turns out to be a hearing deputy coroner who feigned deafness.

Title: An Italian Straw Hat
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *On the way to the wedding, the bridegroom's horse eats a lady's hat... one of which revolves about a deaf man, Uncle Vezinet (Olivier). He is oblivious to noise...

Title: The Ridin' Rowdy
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): This is another film in which a hearing character feigns deafness.

Title: The Flying Marine
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Injured performing movie stunts, a former marine pilot (Lyon) loses his hearing... When his hearing returns, he learns of his girlfriend's love for his brother... Lyon continues his stunt flying, which results in his death.

Title: The Dummy
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Ruth Chatterton and Frederick March star as the parents of the kidnapped daughter; Bennett plays the young detective who feigns deafness to infiltrate the gang of kidnappers.

Title: Beau Bandit
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Rod LaRoque plays a Hispanic bandit... His sidekick is the deaf-mute Colosso. LaRoque uses a few primitive signs and gestures, and Colosso survives by lip-reading...

Title: Mountain Justice
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Maynard plays a cowboy whose father is shot after receiving a warning note. He feigns deafness to force individuals to communicate through notes...

Title: Sarah and Son
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Ruth Chatterton and Fredric March star... the new parents attempt to trick her by substituting a deaf child, but they do not succeed.

Title: The Man Who Played God
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *See film no. 11. In this version, Royal, the concert pianist, remains deaf. Also, the film features an incredible lip-reading test...

Title: Charlie Chan at the Olympics
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *A secret radio-aeoguidance device is stolen... One of the competitors hires a deaf man to lip-read a conversation between Chan and one of the foreign spies, using high-powered binoculars.

Title: Sins of Man
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Jean Hersholt stars as an Austrian bellringer. His wife dies in childbirth and Hersholt seeks help for the child, who is deaf... The child's hearing is restored in a World War I accident.

Title: The Story of Alexander Graham Bell
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This is primarily the story of Bell's invention of the telephone... Young stars as Mabel Hubbard, an oral deaf woman who lip-reads perfectly and speaks clearly.

Title: And Now Tomorrow
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Young stars as a beautiful, rich, deaf woman... Alan Ladd plays a young man... who becomes a doctor... experimental drugs... eventually cure her deafness.

Title: Out of the Past
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *A gangster (Kirk Douglas) confronts a gas station owner (Robert Mitchum)... A 'deaf kid' works for Mitchum and they communicate via signs and finger spelling...

Title: Johnny Belinda
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This is the story of an illiterate deaf woman, known locally as the 'dummy'... A sympathetic physician (Lew Ayres) takes an interest in her and teaches her signs and finger spelling... Wyman remains mute throughout the film.

Title: Moonrise
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Danny Hawkins (Dane Clark) is taunted throughout his childhood... When Danny accidentally kills one of the town bullies, he is distraught to find the murder weapon in the hands of a deaf-mute named Billy Scripture (Morgan).

Title: No Way Out
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Sidney Poitier plays a black doctor... Another brother is a deaf-mute (Bellaver) who reads comic books and can lip-read; the two brothers and the deceased brother's wife (Linda Darnell) all use signs...

Title: Crash of Silence (aka Mandy)
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This English drama portrays a family's effort to cope with their deaf child... After a brush with death... her mother takes her to a residential school... The climax of the film has Mandy finally talking.

Title: The Big Sky
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This western is one of several films that feature Indians who communicate in the sign language of the Plains Indians. Elizabeth Threatt is featured as a Blackfoot Indian princess who does not speak English but occasionally uses signs.

Title: Walk East on Beacon
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Lip-reading is featured in this quasi-documentary account of the capture of a spy ring... Students at the 'Plummer School of Lip-reading' read the lips of foreign spies...

Title: All of Me
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): This is a biographical sketch of the popular hard-of-hearing singer Johnny Ray.

Title: Flesh and Fury (aka Hear No Evil)
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): This is the story of a deaf boxer, Dummy Callan (Curtis), who initially does not speak or use signs... Callan later undergoes an operation that restores his hearing... During the championship fight, Callan again loses his hearing...

Title: The Green Scarf
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This English film is set in France, where a deaf-blind man (Moore), who has written a successful autobiography, is accused of murder... Finger spelling into the hand is used in the film but is unintelligible.

Title: The Story of Esther Costello
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This film depicts the story of a young woman (Sears) who becomes deaf and blind as the result of a bomb explosion... The husband rapes Esther, which so traumatizes her that her hearing, vision, and speech are restored.

Title: Sincerely Yours
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This film is a remake of The Man Who Played God... the main character's hearing loss is the result of otosclerosis; thus, he regains his hearing in mid-picture only to lose it again. The film concludes with a successful fenestration operation.

Title: Shield for Murder
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): A detective (Edmund O'Brien) kills a bookie and steals $25,000. An old deaf-mute (Myer) witnesses the killing and is murdered after he reports it to the police.

Title: The Prodigal
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *The prodigal son, Michah (Edmund Purdom), saves the runaway slave, Asham (Cameron Mitchell), a mute... Deaf publications incorrectly reported the mute character as deaf...

Title: No Road Back
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *A blind-deaf London nightclub owner (Rawlings) lets a gang use her club... She locks the leader in her office and, with the aid of her guide dog, shoots at him... she is killed in the process.

Title: Man of a Thousand Faces
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This biographical film of the silent film star Lon Chaney (James Cagney), whose parents (Lovsky and Leary) were deaf, provides good insights into a deaf family and outsiders' reactions to deafness.

Title: The Proud Rebel
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Alan Ladd stars as a former Civil War rebel who seeks medical help for his son David, a mute, who uses signs and speaks in an impaired voice... David later speaks his father's name and saves him from an ambush.

Title: Maracaibo
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Cornel Wilde plays a firefighter hired to put out an offshore oil fire. The oil well owner is a mute (Lederer) who communicates via signs. His aide... interprets for him.

Title: The Tingler
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *The deaf-mute owner of a theater that shows silent movies communicates with her husband via finger spelling... The diabolical pathologist 'discovers' that unless human fear can be relieved by a scream... he intentionally frightens the theater owner (Evelyn)...

Title: For the First Time
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Tony Costa (Mario Lanza) is an infamous opera singer. He falls in love with a young woman (von Koszian) who is deaf but lip-reads and speaks perfectly; she refuses to marry him unless she can hear.

Title: The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *In one episode two river con artists... pretend to be an English minister accompanied by his deaf brother... The Duke parodies signs... When the real English cousins arrive, they communicate in correct signs...

Title: Happiness of Us Alone
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Takamine and Kobayashi play a deaf couple who use Japanese signs. Although their first child dies as a result of their inability to hear, the film is sympathetic... distributors advertised it as 'in the Johnny Belinda' tradition.

Title: Mumu
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): This film, about loneliness and the need for love, features a huge deaf-mute field hand who is brought to Moscow to work... He names the dog 'Mumu,' the only word he can articulate.

Title: Pocketful of Miracles
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *The film depicts a band of Broadway peddlers... a deaf flower peddler (Corby)... The deaf flower peddler uses authentic sign language but must read lips throughout the film since no one else signs to her.

Title: The Miracle Worker
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Anne Sullivan's pedagogy consists of continuous finger spelling into Helen Keller's hand until the young deaf-blind girl achieves understanding... The symbol of this understanding... is depicted in the utterance 'wah-wah' (water).

Title: Lassie's Great Adventure
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Lassie and his young master are carried away in a hot-air balloon to Canada... A deaf-mute Indian, Chinook Pete (Kiel), discovers them and decides to replace his dead son with Lassie's master.

Title: Violent Midnight
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): This mystery includes a deaf-mute chauffeur, who is one of four murder suspects.

Title: Hollywood Nudes Report
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): A publisher enters the nude movie business, and after interviewing several beautiful women, he selects a deaf-mute as his first star.

Title: Nine Miles to Noon
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): An American travels to Greece to find the wife and child he deserted years earlier... The son's friend is a 'deaf-mute.'

Title: Casanova '70
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *The Casanova of 1970 (Marcello Mastroianni) is impotent and can be aroused sexually only by danger... he woos the wife of a jealous aristocrat who feigns deafness...

Title: Ballad of Love
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Winner of a Moscow Festival short subject film award, this is an English-subtitled Russian love story about a hearing musician... and a dancer (Fyodorova) who has been deaf since the age of three...

Title: Mirage
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Several references mention deaf-blindness in connection with this film starring Gregory Peck; however, there are no deaf-blind or deaf characters.

Title: Weekend of Fear
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): The actors do not speak in this low-budget film; narration is dubbed... The plot centers around a middle-aged woman who hires a deaf-mute to kill the girlfriend of the man she desires.

Title: Who's Minding the Mint?
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): This comedy centers on a plot to break into the U.S. Mint and print duplicate bills. A hard-of-hearing safecracker is a member of the gang.

Title: Psych-Out
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This film depicts the life-style, music, and drug scene of San Francisco's flower children. A young, deaf runaway (Strasberg) who is searching for her brother speaks clearly, reads lips perfectly, and does not use sign language.

Title: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter
Deaf Character(s) Played By: Alan Arkin; Chuck McCann; Horace Oats Jr.
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *The film deals with loneliness and alienation in a small southern town. The chief symbol of loneliness is a deaf jeweler, John Singer (Arkin)... Singer's only true friend is another deaf man... When his deaf friend is sent to an institution... Singer despairs and commits suicide.

Title: Our Silent Love
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): A young Japanese man, the son of deaf parents, cannot find employment. A factory owner, who has a deaf daughter, offers him a job... The deaf couple attempt suicide but are saved...

Title: Koshish
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This Indian (Hindi) film depicts the life of a deaf man, Hari (Kumar), and a deaf woman, Aarti (Bhaduri)... Aarti and Hari... marry... Many years later Aarti dies... the film closes with his finger-spelled proposal to Padma.

Title: The Pleasure Game
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): An impotent father satisfies his sexual needs by watching others through peepholes. On one occasion he brings home two women, one of whom is deaf.

Title: Hot Boarding House
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): For other examples of deaf characters in sex-oriented films...

Title: Private Arrangement
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): For other examples of deaf characters in sex-oriented films...

Title: Tarzan's Deadly Silence
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): Tarzan is temporarily deafened as the result of grenades thrown into a river while he is engaged in a fight.

Title: Fuzz
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This film depicts the success of the 'inept' detectives of the 87th Precinct... Two hearing-impaired characters are featured: Teddy Carella (Adams) is the deaf-mute wife... the 'deaf-man' (Brynner) is a murderer and extortionist...

Title: Deaf Smith and Johnny Ears
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): In this Italian-made western, Quinn plays a deaf-mute war veteran who ekes out a living as a hired gun.

Title: Harry in Your Pocket
Deaf Character(s) Played By: Robert Peace; Arthur Horan; Margaret Horan; Don Peace; Carolyn Peace; Freda Harlander
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Local deaf people are used in an opening scene at the Seattle-Takoma Airport... One of the deaf persons is the victim of a pickpocket.

Title: Badlands
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Martin Sheen and Sissy Spacek star... In one scene they seek refuge and supplies at a rich man's house, when the deaf maid allows them access to the house...

Title: Tommy
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Tommy is a 'deaf, dumb, and blind' character who becomes a world champion pinball-machine player... No sign language or finger spelling is used... Tommy does give a gesture-song sermon... it is all a parody of signs.

Title: Nashville
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This is a cynical look at the country music industry... One of the leading characters is played by Lily Tomlin, a gospel singer and the mother of two deaf children, both of whom use authentic Manual English and speak with hearing-impaired voices.

Title: Against a Crooked Sky
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *A teenage girl is kidnapped by Indians and her brother teams up with a feisty prospector (Richard Boone) and a mute Indian chief, Cut-Tongue, who uses Indian sign language, to find her.

Title: Looking for Mr. Goodbar
Deaf Character(s) Played By: Local deaf children and deaf adults
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Diane Keaton stars as Theresa... By day, Theresa is a competent teacher of deaf children; by night, she cruises the singles bars of Manhattan... There are numerous opportunities to observe signed English, interpreting, and speech pedagogy...

Title: Voices
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *Rosemarie Lemon (Irving), a deaf teacher of deaf children, is engaged to a deaf man (Kendall), but she meets and falls in love with a hearing man... This is the first film in which an actor (Irving) attempts a hearing-impaired voice...

Title: Amy
Deaf Character(s) Played By: Otto Rechenberg and other unidentified deaf students
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *The setting for this film is a residential school for deaf and blind children at the turn of the century... A young teacher, Amy (Jenny Agutter)... establishes an 'articulation' program (speech and lip-reading)... With the presence of Fant... Fabray... and real deaf children... the signs and finger spelling are clear and correct.

Title: Eyes of a Stranger
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *A Miami television broadcaster becomes increasingly alarmed about a rapist... her younger sister, Tracy (Leigh) is deaf, blind, and mute as a result of a childhood rape... In the end, the killer finds Tracy... which triggers the return of her hearing and sight.

Title: The Boat Is Full
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): In this film, a German Jew feigns deafness to avoid expulsion from Switzerland to Germany.

Title: Black Moon Rising
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This is the story of the effort to steal and recover a high-tech automobile. One of the helpers of the car's owner is deaf, and he is killed when he cannot hear the villain's car approaching.

Title: Children of a Lesser God
Deaf Character(s) Played By: Marlee Matlin; Linda Bove; William Byrd; Frank Carter Jr.; John Cleary; Georgia Ann Cline; Alison Gompf; Bob Hiltermann; Philip Holmes; John Limnidis
Synopsis (as written in Filmography): *This love story involves a speech teacher (William Hurt) and a nonspeaking deaf woman (Matlin) who signs and is extremely suspicious of all persons who hear. All of the deaf characters either have understandable speech or are voice-interpreted... and the signs are authentic.
